name: Marbled Chocolate Cheesecake (Lo Cal)
recipeIngredient: 2 eggs, yolks and whites separated, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
In a small saucepan over medium high heat, bring 2/3 cup of milk to a boil. Set aside.
In a blender container, sprinkle gelatin over remaining 1/4 cup cold skim milk. Let stand for 4 to 5 minutes.
Add boiling skim milk; cover and blend at low speed for 2 minutes.
Add 6 tablespoons sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla extract; blend at medium speed until well mixed.
Add cottage cheese; blend at high speed until smooth.
Remove 3/4 cup of the mixture to use for marbling. Chill until the mixture begins to thicken.
In a bowl, combine 1 tablespoon sugar and cocoa. Add to blender container and blend until mixed.
Pour chocolate mixture into a large bowl. Chill, stirring occasionally, until the mixture mounds when dropped from a spoon.
In a small bowl with an electric mixer at high speed, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add remaining 2 tablespoons sugar; beat until stiff peaks form.
Fold beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture and pour into the prepared crust.
Spoon large dollops of vanilla mixture over the chocolate layer. With a knife or spatula, gently swirl to create a marbled effect.
Cover; refrigerate until firm, about 4 to 5 hours.
In a small bowl, combine crumbs and cocoa. Add melted butter and stir until evenly blended. Press mixture evenly on the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
